info.name: Smart Recruitment Platform - Web App
info.description: Web consumer collection for public website visitors, employer dashboard, admin dashboard, and supported web job seeker flows. TODO: add dedicated analytics/reporting endpoints here if the backend later exposes them.

request.description: Web App / Visitor: browse published jobs on the public website.
Web App / Visitor: view public job details.
Web App / Visitor: load public skill reference data.
Web App / Employer: create an employer account, company, and employer profile. Requires accepted terms; optional website and phone are persisted.
Web App / Employer: login and stores the returned Sanctum token in {{employer_token}}.
Web App / Admin: login and stores the returned Sanctum token in {{admin_token}}.
Web App / Employer/Admin: fetch the authenticated web-role user. Uses {{employer_token}} by default.
Web App / Employer/Admin: request a password reset email with generic success to avoid email enumeration.
Web App / Employer/Admin: reset password using a broker token from email or tests. Set {{reset_token}} before sending.
Web App / Employer/Admin: change password while authenticated. Uses {{employer_token}} by default.
Web App / Employer/Admin: revoke the current Sanctum token. Uses {{employer_token}} by default.
Web App / Employer/Admin: revoke all Sanctum tokens for the authenticated web-role user. Uses {{employer_token}} by default.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: load company profile.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: update company profile.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: load employer user profile.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: update employer contact profile.
Returns 422 JOB_REQUIRED_SKILL_MISSING when only optional skills exist.
Returns work_mode, application_deadline, deadline flags, can_apply, and classified skills.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: create a job posting.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: list employer-owned jobs.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: update owned job posting.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: legacy skill_ids remain supported and are treated as required. Prefer the structured skills contract for required/optional classification.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: publish an owned job. Requires the employer company to be approved and creates a job.published audit log.

Web App / Employer Dashboard: pending companies can update their company profile but cannot publish jobs. Expected response is 403 when {{pending_employer_token}} belongs to a pending-company employer.

exec: pm.test('pending company blocked or auth missing', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([401, 403, 404]));

request.description: Web App / Employer Dashboard: close an owned job.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: delete an owned job posting.

Web App / Employer Dashboard: list applications for an owned job. Authorized employer responses retain internal status notes and limited actor summaries; candidate responses intentionally exclude them.

Web App / Employer Dashboard: view an application for an owned job, including internal status notes and limited actor summaries. Candidate responses intentionally exclude internal hiring notes and evaluation details.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: move an application through the pipeline.
Returns 409 APPLICATION_INFORMATION_REQUEST_ALREADY_OPEN when a pending request exists.
An employer from another company receives 403.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: get explainable ranked candidates for a job.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: list test catalog entries available to employer.
Creates a test owned by the authenticated employer's company. company_id is deliberately not accepted from employers.
Updates an own-company test. Returns 409 once the test has any assignment.
Deletes an own-company test only before assignment; otherwise returns 409.
Lists ordered questions and options for an own-company test.
Creates a question and its options atomically. Choice types enforce correct-answer rules.
Shows one question after verifying it belongs to the test.
Updates a question/options before assignment; otherwise returns 409.
Deletes a question before assignment and rejects cross-test identifiers.
Reorders the complete question set. Partial or foreign ID sets are rejected.
Adds an option to a compatible question before assignment.
Updates an option while preserving the question type's complete correctness rules.
Deletes an option only when the remaining set is still valid.
Reorders the complete option set. Partial or foreign ID sets are rejected.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: assign a test to an application.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: view the candidate test submission and its normalized attempt answers through the existing application tests resource.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: evaluate a submitted test attempt.
Employer reads normalized answers only for an application owned by the employer's company.
Detailed own-company attempt result with explainable breakdown.
Objective-only example: exact-set grading, final percentage, and an informational absolute-points passing indicator.

item.name: View Mixed Result Requiring Manual Grading

request.description: Mixed example: objective subtotal is available while total, percentage, and passing indicator remain pending.
Shows total, graded, remaining, and complete manual-grading progress.
Shows finalized objective plus manual totals and detailed reviewer breakdown.
Allowed only while the attempt is unsubmitted; the editable environment deadline must be in the future.
Employer/Admin-only history; internal reasons and actor identity are not exposed to candidates.
Use a token from a different company; expected response is 403.
Shows every preserved attempt and manager-only retake metadata without combining results.
Expected 409 after the configured maximum is used.
Expected 409 while the latest assignment is unsubmitted.
Expected 409 when the referenced assignment is not latest.
Use an employer token from another company; expected 403.
Expected 409 for interview/final/terminal application states.
Authorized private download for an own-company candidate submission.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: schedule an interview for an application.

Web App / Employer Dashboard: list application interviews with authorized management notes and evaluation details. Candidate responses intentionally exclude internal hiring notes and evaluation details.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: update an interview.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: mark an interview as completed.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: submit interview evaluation.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: use status change endpoint for final accept/reject decisions. Accepted/rejected final decisions create application.accepted or application.rejected audit records.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: list employer notifications related to the authenticated employer company. Uses {{employer_token}}.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: get unread employer notification count. Uses {{employer_token}}.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: mark one owned employer notification as read. Uses {{employer_token}}.
Web App / Employer Dashboard: mark all current employer notifications as read. Uses {{employer_token}}.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: paginated list of platform users.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: filter platform users by role.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: filter platform users by account status.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: view a user without sensitive tokens or password fields.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: activate a suspended user account.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: suspend a user account and revoke tokens when supported.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: paginated list of companies.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: filter companies by approval status.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: view company detail with employer and activity counts.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: approve a company for employer workflows.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: reject a company approval request.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: suspend company workflow access.

item.name: 14 Admin - Security and Account State
item.description: Central account/company state controls. Suspended users receive USER_SUSPENDED and all their tokens are revoked. Employer recruitment APIs return COMPANY_PENDING, COMPANY_REJECTED, or COMPANY_SUSPENDED while identity/profile access remains available.

request.description: Suspends the user and revokes every Sanctum token. Future protected requests return 403 USER_SUSPENDED.
Reactivates the account without recreating revoked tokens; the user must log in again.
Generic status transition. Setting suspended uses the same all-token revocation path as Suspend User.
Freezes recruitment operations without deleting jobs, applications, interviews, tests, or employer tokens. Employer operations return COMPANY_SUSPENDED.
Enables recruitment operations again. Existing open jobs return to public visibility without status mutation.
Representative guarded endpoint. Pending/rejected/suspended companies return 403 with COMPANY_PENDING, COMPANY_REJECTED, or COMPANY_SUSPENDED.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: list and search reference skills.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: create a reusable skill reference.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: update a reusable skill reference.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: safely delete unused skills; used skills return a conflict instead of breaking relationships.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: list assessment tests.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: create assessment test.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: update assessment test.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: delete assessment test.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: list admin audit log entries.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: filter audit logs by action.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: basic read-only JSON statistics for users, companies, jobs, applications, tests, interviews, notifications, CVs, and audit logs.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: read-only application status and daily counts.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: read-only job status counts and average applications per job.
Web App / Admin Dashboard: read-only CV parsing and profile suggestion counts.
